---
title: "Goodbyes"
date: 2026-07-20
---

My seniors graduated today, and tbh, it was heartbreaking. It hurts to realize I won’t see them around anymore, much less get to talk to them. Some of them were my campus parents too. Yet, I couldn't even give them a proper send off, I literally just said "Bye!" and walked away. I had expected myself to give everyone this grand, personalized speech about how amazing they are and how we'd somehow cross paths again. But nope. Maybe I’m just not that great with goodbyes.

It got me thinking: conclusions don't have to be happy or sad, mad or unnerving. Endings are just that - endings. Everything that has a beginning has an end. It’s just in our innate nature as humans to humanize everything. Maybe I am sad, mad, and happy all at once. Maybe I'm none of those things. Everything comes to an end, even connections.

*Maybe connections are a waste of time, though the pool of tears I'm currently lying in would probably prove me wrong.*
